perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means all financial, business and other information, in whatever form or medium (whether written, oral or electronic), including, without limitation, any trade secrets, processes, financial data, technical data and documentation, strategic planning, product/service specifications, prototypes, computer programs, drawings, models, customer information and marketing data that is furnished or disclosed by a Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party solely in connection with the Evaluation, except that such term will not include (i) information already known by the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality, (ii) information that is or becomes publicly known other than through a breach by the Receiving Party of any of its obligations under this Agreement, (iii) information received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not known by the Receiving Party to be under an obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party, (iv) information independently developed by the Receiving Party without reliance upon the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and (v) information that the Disclosing Party has disclosed to other parties without similar obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means all financial, business and other information, in whatever form or medium (whether written, oral or electronic), including, without limitation, any trade secrets, processes, financial data, technical data and documentation, strategic planning, product/service specifications, prototypes, computer programs, drawings, models, customer information and marketing data that is furnished or disclosed by a Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party solely in connection with the Evaluation, and including (i) information already known by the Receiving Party without an obligation of confidentiality, (ii) information that is or becomes publicly known other than through a breach by the Receiving Party of any of its obligations under this Agreement, (iii) information received by the Receiving Party from a third party who is not known by the Receiving Party to be under an obligation of confidence to the Disclosing Party, (iv) information independently developed by the Receiving Party without reliance upon the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and (v) information that the Disclosing Party has disclosed to other parties without similar obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "except that such term will not include" to "and including" in the definition of Confidential Information, information previously excluded from the definition is now explicitly included. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 8 discusses Independent Product Development Not Affected, which relies on excluding independently developed information from the definition of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 8. Competition; Independent Product Development Not Affected. The parties hereto acknowledge that they may compete in certain lines of business. Nothing herein shall be construed to prevent the parties from continuing to so compete or from continuing to carry out their respective plans and objectives, provided that the parties comply with the terms of this Agreement. Further, the terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either party's right to independently develop or acquire products, functionality or services of the same type as may be included within any Confidential Information or to enter into any business transaction with any other company which owns or has rights to any such similar products or services, as long as such right is exercised without the use of any other party's Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement. In addition, each party agrees that any demonstration, description, or disclosure of its products, services, or methods to the other party, including but not limited to any functionality of its products, services, or methods (“Product Information”), is not, and will not be considered, Confidential Information hereunder (other than with respect to the non-disclosure obligations contained herein). Each party further agrees that any demonstration, description, or disclosure of Product Information to the other party will not form the basis for any action by the Company against ADP, or ADP against the Company, for breach of this Agreement ((other than with respect to the non-disclosure obligations contained herein), breach of contract, or misappropriation of trade secrets.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excluded certain types of information from the definition of "Confidential Information." The changed text now includes these types of information, specifically (i) through (v), in the definition, which directly contradicts Section 8. Section 8 relies on excluding independently developed information (which would fall under (iv)) from the definition of confidential information to allow for independent product development. By including this information, the change makes it unclear whether independent product development is still allowed without violating the confidentiality agreement, creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, where a clause is modified with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace, and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information may be used for any purpose the Receiving Party deems appropriate, including Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of confidential information to a specific purpose (the Evaluation). The modification removes this restriction, allowing the Receiving Party to use the information for 'any purpose', which directly contradicts the confidentiality obligation detailed in Section 3 that limits its use to the stated purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3. Confidentiality Obligation. Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above), disclose or otherwise make available any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person other than Representatives of the Receiving Party who have a business need to know Mutual Non-­‐Disclosure Agreement Version 2 2 for purposes of the Evaluation, and who have been advised by the Receiving Party of the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion. The Receiving Party will be responsible for the compliance of its Representatives with the terms of this Agreement. If a Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, court order or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement and the Receiving Party will request that all Confidential Information so disclosed is treated confidentially (and any such disclosure by the Receiving Party will not violate the terms of this Agreement). The Disclosing Party shall reimburse the Receiving Party for all of its reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the terms of the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the restriction on how the Receiving Party can use the Confidential Information, stating it 'may be used for any purpose the Receiving Party deems appropriate.' This directly contradicts Section 3, which explicitly limits the use of Confidential Information to the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party of any breach or unauthorized use directly contradicts Section 3, which places a confidentiality obligation on the Receiving Party and implies a responsibility to protect the Disclosing Party's information. Failing to report a breach undermines this duty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3. Confidentiality Obligation. Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above), disclose or otherwise make available any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person other than Representatives of the Receiving Party who have a business need to know Mutual Non-­‐Disclosure Agreement Version 2 2 for purposes of the Evaluation, and who have been advised by the Receiving Party of the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion. The Receiving Party will be responsible for the compliance of its Representatives with the terms of this Agreement. If a Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, court order or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement and the Receiving Party will request that all Confidential Information so disclosed is treated confidentially (and any such disclosure by the Receiving Party will not violate the terms of this Agreement). The Disclosing Party shall reimburse the Receiving Party for all of its reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the terms of the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes the Receiving Party's obligation to notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts Section 3, which places a confidentiality obligation on the Receiving Party and outlines specific responsibilities, including treating the information as confidential and taking reasonable care to protect it. By removing the notification requirement, the Receiving Party is no longer bound to inform the Disclosing Party of potential breaches, undermining the confidentiality obligation outlined in Section 3. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for three years from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect indefinitely from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term length from a fixed period of three years to indefinitely creates a direct contradiction. This affects Section 6, which addresses reproduction and return of confidential information, implying a finite timeframe for those actions, now with the term modified to be indefinite, creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6. Reproduction and Return of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party will not reproduce the Confidential Information except as reasonably necessary for purposes of the Evaluation. If the Receiving Party reproduces all or any part of any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will not remove or obscure any confidential or proprietary notices or legends, if any, that appear in the originals thereof. At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information and any reproductions thereof. The authorized officer of the Receiving Party that is responsible for such return or destruction shall deliver a written certification to the Disclosing Party of such return or destruction. The Receiving Party’s obligations under Sections 2 and 3 will survive any return or destruction of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement's duration from a fixed three years to indefinitely. Section 6 outlines obligations related to confidential information, including its return or destruction, implying a finite period. By changing the agreement to indefinite, it is unclear when or if the return/destruction obligation would be triggered, thus creating a contradiction as the finite actions described in Section 6 are at odds with an indefinite agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party hereto without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be assigned by either party hereto without the need for prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for written consent for assignment, this directly contradicts the original clause. This has implications in Section 12. Miscellaneous, which states the agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 12. Miscellaneous. This Agreement may not be changed, modified or amended except by a writing signed by each party to this Agreement. This Agreement may not be discharged except by performance in accordance with its terms. This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party hereto without the prior written consent of the other party. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties as to the subject matter hereof and merges and supersedes all prior discussions, agreements and understandings of any kind and every nature, whether written or oral, between them. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to contracts wholly made and performed in such state. This Agreement may be signed in two or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'This Agreement may not be assigned by either party hereto without the prior written consent of the other party.' The changed text modifies this to 'This Agreement may be assigned by either party hereto without the need for prior written consent of the other party.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text in Section 12, which includes the clause 'This Agreement may not be assigned by either party hereto without the prior written consent of the other party.' The change removes a restriction (prior written consent), which is in direct conflict with the unedited statement of restriction in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees not to use any trade name, service mark, or trademark of the other party or refer to the other party in any promotional activity or material without first obtaining the prior written consent of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party is encouraged to use any trade name, service mark, or trademark of the other party and refer to the other party in any promotional activity or material without first obtaining the prior written consent of such party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits the use of trade names, service marks, or trademarks without prior consent. The modified version encourages their use without consent, creating a direct contradiction. This directly contradicts section 9. No Implied License which previously restricted such activity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9. No Implied License. Except as otherwise provided herein, nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute an implied license in favor of either party to any proprietary rights of the other party, including, without limitation, any patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. Each party agrees not to use any trade name, service mark, or trademark of the other party or refer to the other party in any promotional activity or material without first obtaining the prior written consent of such party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and section 9. The original text prohibits using the other party's trademarks without consent, while the changed text encourages it without consent. This creates a direct conflict with section 9's clause stating 'Each party agrees not to use any trade name, service mark, or trademark of the other party or refer to the other party in any promotional activity or material without first obtaining the prior written consent of such party,' making the obligations unclear and unenforceable.
